channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Stanford's 2026 AI Index just dropped. Junior developer employment is down 20%. Here's what the data actually says.
item.description: The Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI released its 2026 AI Index today. It is the most comprehensive annual measurement of where AI actually stands — not where the press releases say it...

item.title: Trunk-Based Development vs Git Flow
item.description: Trunk-Based Development vs. Git Flow A decision document comparing two branching strategies. Evaluates each approach against our CI/CD maturity, team velocity, and DevOps best practices — then...

item.title: Python Developer AI Toolkit, Part 2: Five CLI scripts that automate the prompts
item.description: Part 1 of this series covered the prompt library — 272 prompts for Python and backend development organized by task type. This is Part 2: the five CLI scripts that turn those prompts into...

item.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent building tool for enterprises
item.description: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: it is geared for IT and technical users.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos rollout has missed America’s cybersecurity agency
item.description: Several US federal agencies are taking up Anthropic's new cybersecurity model to find vulnerabilities, but one is reportedly not getting in on the action: the nation's central cybersecurity...

item.title: Vibe Coding vs a Senior Dev: 5 Prompts, One App Clone
item.description: There's a video on YouTube where a vibe coder and a senior iOS developer - ten years of experience - both try to clone Granola, a $250M AI meeting notetaker, using only five prompts each. No...

item.title: We Like to Benchmark AI, But What If We've Been Using a Ruler to Measure Weight This Whole Time?
item.description: Every few months, a new leaderboard drops. MMLU scores. HumanEval. GPQA. Models get ranked, Twitter erupts, someone declares AGI is two weeks away, and we all move on. But here's the thing that's...

item.title: How I Built a Privacy-First PDF Tool Using pdf-lib and the Canvas API
item.description: A few weeks ago I got frustrated with online PDF tools. Not with their functionality — iLovePDF and Smallpdf are genuinely useful. What frustrated me was what happens to your files after you click...

item.title: Should we feel guilty for using AI?
item.description: This blog post is adapted from a blog I wrote at work - it focusses on AI usage in a work setting, particularly from my perspective as a software engineer. Introduction How do you feel about ‘AI’...

item.title: How Rideshare Drivers Track 1099 Income and Schedule C Deductions in 2026
item.description: It's February. You just got your 1099-K from Uber, your 1099-NEC from DoorDash, and a weekly summary from Lyft sitting in three different email threads. You open a shoebox full of gas receipts,...

item.title: SQL SET Operators: UNION, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT Explained Simply
item.description: If you are just starting out with SQL, you have probably already learned how to write a SELECT query, filter data with WHERE, and maybe even join two tables together. But what happens when you...

item.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
item.description: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

item.title: Sibling Rivalry? How to Make Kestra Tasks Talk to Each Other
item.description: I'm putting together a video on sibling tasks in Kestra and thought I would build this idea out loud with you in a blog first. Let's jump in! What are sibling tasks? Sibling tasks are tasks that...

item.title: Microsoft teases mysterious Discord and Xbox Game Pass partnership
item.description: Microsoft has started teasing a new Discord and Xbox Game Pass partnership, just a day after lowering prices of its Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscriptions. Microsoft Gaming CEO Asha...

item.title: Google Meet will take AI notes for in-person meetings too
item.description: Google's AI meeting notetaker is no longer limited to Google Meets - Gemini can also generate summaries and transcripts of in-person meetings now, as well as meetings on Zoom and Microsoft Teams,...

item.title: Microsoft says the ‘idea’ of an Xbox mobile store ‘is not dead’
item.description: Microsoft first revealed it was building an Xbox mobile gaming store to take on Apple and Google all the way back in 2022. Former Xbox president Sarah Bond even went on stage at a Bloomberg event...

item.title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
item.description: One group of hackers used AI for everything from vibe coding their malware to creating fake company websites—and stole as much as $12 million in three months.

item.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
Will a new CEO help realize Apple’s smart home potential?
item.description: It took Tim Cook years to launch Apple into major new hardware categories, such as the smartwatch. But John Ternus could start his tenure right away with an ambitious new project: smart home...

item.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
item.description: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the...

item.title: Sony’s PlayStation 5 is $200 off for the first time since December
item.description: When the price increases for PlayStation 5 consoles went into effect on April 2nd, we weren’t sure when the next time a good discount would happen. Surely, not for at least a couple months, right?...

item.title: Now Meta will track what employees do on their computers to train its AI agents
item.description: Meta employees' activity at work is now being used to train the company's AI agents. As reported by Reuters, Meta is installing a tool it calls Model Capability Initiative (MCI) on US-based...

item.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
item.description: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows, and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy...

item.title: I bought Alienware’s $350 OLED monitor and I can’t believe how good it is
item.description: I've recommended several OLED gaming monitors to readers over the years, and I've finally taken my own advice to buy one. Alienware's new 27-inch 1440p QD-OLED has all the features that I want and...

item.title: Call of Duty never made much sense for Xbox Game Pass
item.description: Yesterday Microsoft announced some surprising news: at a time when everything in gaming is getting more expensive, Xbox Game Pass Ultimate was actually getting a price cut. Going forward, the...

item.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
item.description: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

item.title: BMW’s flagship 7 Series gets its ‘Neue Klasse’ upgrade
item.description: Ever since BMW first announced its "Neue Klasse" next-generation electric vehicle architecture and design language way back in 2021, the question on many fans' minds was when the new technology...

item.title: The Download: introducing the 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now What...

item.title: The most interesting startups showcased at Google Cloud Next 2026
item.description: Google wants AI startups on its cloud and has showcased a long list of them at its annual conference.

item.title: Google Maps is about to get a big dose of AI
item.description: The new features, announced at Cloud Next in Las Vegas this week, add generative AI capabilities to Google's mapping platform, giving it enhanced visual and data analytics powers.
